place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Fitness First One George is my favorite branch by far. The space is beautifully well-lit in the mornings, with a great view that really sets the mood for a good workout. You can even get a bit of sun without being bothered, which is a nice bonus.

The staff, Yogesh and her team,  trainers and cleaners are consistently friendly and approachable, and the place is always kept tidy. Locker rooms are clean and well-maintained, making the overall experience comfortable from start to finish.

The location is also very convenient. Just know your timings, and you’ll have a great workout every time. Definitely a branch I keep coming back to.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
This studio has a nice view and even gets good sunlight—perfect if you enjoy a little post-workout suntanning.

The equipment selection is complete. The floor plan is a bit small, but during off-peak hours it feels comfortable and easy to move around.
Shower and sauna areas are clean and well maintained.

The personal trainers are polite and considerate of members who train on their own.

Shout out to Dasha and the front desk team for always being friendly and welcoming. They really set the tone when you walk in!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast area and wanted to share my thoughts on East Coast Park. It remains one of the best-maintained spaces in Singapore, and its extensive connectivity to other parks makes it an ideal destination for long walks or cycling trips. I particularly enjoyed the ability to ride my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay via the park connector; it was always such a calm and refreshing experience.

The park offers a wonderful atmosphere with its blend of lush greenery and sea views. It is exceptionally well-equipped for families and fitness enthusiasts, featuring dedicated exercise stations, children’s play areas, and wide, separate paths for cycling and walking that prevent the space from feeling crowded.

Beyond the scenery, the facilities are excellent. There are convenient bicycle rentals, a variety of dining options in the restaurant area where you can enjoy the sea breeze, and well-maintained BBQ pits and shelters. For those interested in staying longer, the designated camping areas are a great touch, and I’ve always found the washrooms and bathing facilities to be very clean.

Overall, it is a perfect place to relax, exercise, and enjoy nature, whether you are visiting alone or with friends and family.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast Park is one of the best and most well-maintained parks in Singapore. It is a very big park and is beautifully connected to other parks across Singapore, making it perfect for long walks or cycling trips. The park has lots of nice scenery, with greenery and sea views that make the atmosphere very relaxing. In some areas, there are exercise equipment stations and children’s play areas, which are great for families and fitness lovers.

There is a dedicated cycling path and a separate walking path, both of which are wide and well maintained, so it never feels crowded. You can also rent bicycles, and there are fun activities and attractions that bring in many people. The park has a restaurant area where you can enjoy a variety of foods while feeling the cool sea breeze. You can book BBQ pits, and there are huts available for shelter in case of rain. There are also designated camping areas for those who want to stay overnight.

I used to live near the East Coast area, and what I loved most was riding my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay, as the park connector makes this possible. It was always a calm and refreshing experience. On top of that, the washrooms and bathing areas are very clean and well maintained. Overall, East Coast Park is a perfect place to relax, exercise, eat, and enjoy nature, whether alone, with friends, or with family.

This is one of the most beautiful parks I’ve ever visited in Singapore.

It has plenty of trees, clean walking paths, and comfortable benches where people can relax and enjoy the peaceful atmosphere. I especially recommend visiting in the morning because the weather is cooler and the park feels very refreshing

There are also many trash bins around the park, so it is convenient for visitors who bring drinks or snacks. The area is well maintained, clean, and perfect for taking a walk, reading a book, or simply spending quiet time away from the busy city. 🌳

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
This MRT station is a shining example of modern technology and convenience! From the sleek architecture to the state-of-the-art ticketing systems, everything is designed for efficiency and comfort.

The platform is spotless, with clear digital displays showing train schedules and real-time updates. The station is also well-ventilated and features energy-efficient lighting, which adds to its futuristic vibe.

One of the best things is the seamless integration of facilities – plenty of escalators, elevators for accessibility, and even charging stations for electronic devices. The station also boasts great connectivity to nearby amenities like shopping malls and parks, making it super convenient for commuters.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🏬 Suntec City – 4.5 stars

Suntec City is one of those malls in Singapore that feels huge but still easy to walk around. There’s a good mix of shopping, restaurants, cafés, and even office spaces, so it’s always lively but not overwhelming.

What I like is the variety — you can find everything from affordable eats to nicer dining spots, plus fashion, lifestyle stores, and specialty coffee places. It’s very convenient if you’re spending the whole day in the area.

The layout is quite spacious, and it connects well to nearby attractions and MRT, which makes it practical for both locals and tourists. It’s not the most luxurious mall in Singapore, but it’s reliable, comfortable, and has everything you need in one place.

Overall, a solid shopping and dining destination. Great for casual hangouts, meetings, or just walking around and exploring. ✨

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
A complex of Fountain of Wealth, Convention & Exhibition Centre, Shopping Mall & 5 Towers of Business Centres.

"Fountain of Wealth
(The heart of Suntec City)
Visit the heart of Suntec City - the stunning Fountain of Wealth, a symbol of wealth and life. Recognised in 1998 by the Guinness Book of World Records as the World's Largest Fountain, the bronze ring of the fountain is designed based on the Hindu Mandala (or "universe") and is a symbolic representation of the oneness in spirit and unity, representing the quality and harmony of all races and religions. Soak in the positive “qi” energy at the fountain by encircling the fountain clockwise three times, stretching out your right hand over the water, as you make a silent wish and let the wonders unfold."

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: We went to Marinna Square Mall to have our HongKong Tim Sum today. We ordered a total of 6 dishes with 1 dessert. He had porridge,Chee cheong fun, fried carrot cake,pandan leaf rice,prawns balls,spring roll and mango sago dessert. These are just nice for 2 of us. It costed us a total of $37. If we had these in any restaurant it will be at least $80..Value for money and the Tim sum is also good and nice. You guys should try and save your pocket. Have added a few photos for your reference. Have also given you the menu too. Enjoyed. Please note it is not a restaurant it just a food outlet in the mall..just right at the end of the roll restuarants there in the mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: If you are in Singapore for F1 next year, Marina Square is the place to be if you don't have tickets. At the rooftop gardens it offers a slight view, and you can get pretty close. Also on the walkways between the malls there was sometimes a view. The sound was incredible. Great for next year!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Marina Square is a bustling local favorite! Visited on a Saturday and the energy was high with multiple expos in full swing. It’s packed with unique small shops and a massive variety of eateries. Love the dedicated wings for kids, maternity, and travel—it makes navigating this huge mall much easier. Great weekend vibe!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
​If you're looking for the most scenic library in Singapore, this is it. Located on the 3rd floor of VivoCity, library@harbourfront is a masterclass in modern, functional design.
​Why it’s a must-visit for me.  The floor-to-ceiling windows offer a breathtaking, panoramic view of the Sentosa waterfront and the cable cars. Snagging one of the yellow deck chairs facing the water is a total game-changer.
​
Despite being in one of Singapore’s busiest malls, it is remarkably peaceful and quiet. The "coastal theme" with its undulating ceilings and blue-green hues makes the entire space feel like a sanctuary.
​
I love the clear distinction between the Adults/Teens zone and the Children's area. It keeps the reading lounges tranquil while the kids have their own interactive "Tinker Truck" and AR books to explore.

It's modern tech, highly efficient with digital newspaper stations, "Learning Pods" for TED Talks, and an easy Grab-n-Go system.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Visited Library @ HarbourFront — one of the nicest local libraries to relax and enjoy some quiet time with a book.
The view overlooking Sentosa Island adds to the calming ambiance, making it a great spot to unwind.

It’s mostly a self-service library with everything fully automated, from borrowing to returning books.
The setup is modern and efficient, making the whole experience smooth and convenient.
A great place for both readers and those looking for a peaceful retreat.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
staff are attentive to library users needs:

noticed staff approached,  2grandmas and a lady with infant in hand and her
pre schooler, to park the stroller to the designated area to prevent incidents from any roaming child/children
👍👍👍👍👍

an announcement was made for parents and child/children to participate together for  the workshop ongoing this day - june twenty six

and shortly after,
noticed the workshop organizer - a gentleman in a cap,
reached out to this family to join in.
It was a sweet gesture as a reminder of ongoing activity
🥰🥰🥰🥰🥰

personally, iwas attended by Mr Arthur
for my little request of writing paper.
so prompt he was
when he was manning the shelves
👍👍👍👍👍

ihv found my happy place to have quick reads
📚📚📚

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Really love the library in the middle of a crowded mall. Very happy they have a security gantry to allow people to scan passes to enter. Divided session of young kids and other section is although a hassle but necessary because the children session can be a little noisy. Also love they have the self check out gantry which is cool.

Books selection may not have the widest variety but good enough for town. I find limited seatings among bookshelves area although proper table/seats are scattered all around. I find a lot of people occupied those seats for working, studying or leisure reading. I really don't like the people taking advantage to nap there but the librarian did go around stopping them. The other point that was not pleasant are the seats facing the waterfront. While it is definitely such a good experience reading and facing sentosa, the air was really stuffy and not ventilated. The heat from the glass was intense and the air con wasn't strong enough.

Beautifully furnished library.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Lovely library with organized sections and several areas for study/work. Just a bit hard to find inside vivo City (it's in Lobby F).

Despite being in one of Singapore’s busiest malls, it is remarkably peaceful and quiet. The "coastal theme" with its undulating ceilings and blue-green hues makes the entire space feel like a sanctuary.
​
I love the clear distinction between the Adults/Teens zone and the Children's area. It keeps the reading lounges tranquil while the kids have their own interactive "Tinker Truck" and AR books to explore.

review_text:
The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC)
Review on 20/1/2026

The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC), founded in 1883 and set along the iconic Padang, is one of Singapore’s most historic social‑sports institutions. Its clubhouse offers a wide range of sports, recreation, dining, and event facilities that continue to serve a vibrant and diverse community.

The club’s most striking feature is its grand entrance, framed by two sweeping curved staircases. This graceful, ceremonial design creates a sense of ascending into a place of heritage and dignity. Against the backdrop of the Padang and the Civic District, the entrance stands out as a timeless and memorable architectural landmark.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Private club in central district with roofed warm and cold swimming pool, restaurant, bar with live band and cafe.
Cost environment for private gathering and easily accessible by MRT via City Hall station

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Dinner with an old friend at the SRC'S Chinese Restaurant. Reminiscent of the old days in Singapore, with traditions alive and well. Very pleasant evening 😊😋

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Our family had a wonderful visit to the Singapore Recreation Club (SRC) during their open house! The location right by the Padang is fantastic, and we were impressed that the facilities are truly top-notch. The pool looked inviting, and the entire club was clean and prestigious. It's clear why it's a desirable spot, but be aware that it is indeed for members only. We really enjoyed our temporary visit and appreciated seeing the high quality of the club.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Raffles Place in Singapore is a captivating destination that effortlessly blends historical charm with contemporary vibrancy. Having explored this iconic neighborhood, it is with great pleasure that I award Raffles Place a well-deserved five-star rating.

The centerpiece of Raffles Place is the historic Raffles Place Park, named after Singapore's founding father, Sir Stamford Raffles. This lush green oasis provides a welcome respite amidst the bustling cityscape. The well-manicured lawns, tranquil ponds, and elegant statues create a serene ambiance, inviting visitors to unwind and appreciate the beauty of their surroundings.

The architectural marvels that line Raffles Place showcase the city's dynamic spirit and modernity. Skyscrapers tower above, housing global financial institutions and multinational corporations. The gleaming glass facades and innovative designs exemplify Singapore's status as a global financial hub. As I strolled through the streets, the juxtaposition of historic buildings and futuristic structures offered a unique visual tapestry, symbolizing the city's ability to blend tradition and progress seamlessly.

Raffles Place is not just a business district; it is a cultural and culinary haven. The neighborhood boasts a wide array of dining options that cater to every palate. From trendy cafes and fine dining establishments to local hawker centers, culinary delights await at every turn. Indulge in a gastronomic journey, savoring diverse cuisines from around the world or sampling delectable local dishes. The variety of dining choices ensures that every culinary craving is satisfied.

Shopaholics will find themselves in paradise at Raffles Place. The neighborhood is home to luxury boutiques, high-end malls, and unique specialty stores. From fashion and accessories to electronics and home decor, the retail offerings cater to a range of tastes and preferences. The experience of shopping in Raffles Place is elevated by the impeccable service and attention to detail that Singapore is renowned for.

Raffles Place is also a transportation hub, making it easily accessible from all corners of the city. The efficient MRT (Mass Rapid Transit) system connects Raffles Place to various parts of Singapore, ensuring convenient travel for both residents and visitors. The well-planned pedestrian walkways and underground passages further enhance accessibility, allowing for seamless exploration of the area.

Beyond its commercial prowess, Raffles Place holds historical significance. The district is steeped in colonial heritage, with notable landmarks such as the Old Parliament House and the Asian Civilisations Museum in close proximity. History enthusiasts will appreciate the opportunity to delve into Singapore's past, gaining insights into its cultural roots and evolution.

In conclusion, Raffles Place is a captivating neighborhood that offers a blend of history, modernity, and cultural experiences. Its stunning architecture, culinary delights, and accessibility make it a five-star destination. Whether you're a business traveler, a culture enthusiast, or simply seeking an unforgettable urban experience, Raffles Place in Singapore will leave an indelible impression, showcasing the city's remarkable ability to embrace progress while cherishing its heritage.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: One of the best national libraries I’ve seen, with a large number of books to rent. 24/7 book drop, a lot of magazines available to read, plenty of quiet, comfy and beautiful areas to read in, and that’s all just the basement level – there are 15 floors all with their own content. Floors 5, 10 and 15 have green areas if you’d like to be in a forest-like environment while reading/working/studying. If you’re nearby, surely a great place to visit!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Perhaps the largest public library in Singapore. Hard to find a librarian for assistance, and bookings for seats are required if you do not want to be asked to leave just because someone has booked the seats. A really stupid concept for those who would casually come into the library if they are in the area. Throughout the years, NLB has done nothing to enhance the library experience. What the public has been slapped with are rules and regulations which makes visiting the library a chore.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Came purely for the kids section of the library and it sure lived up to our expectations. The little one thoroughly enjoyed himself amidst the aquarium themed library and the endless literature around the area. Very thoughtfully, they also offer floor cushions where you can take and just find a spot to read and chill out on. The hit area with the deck chairs is the central of the kids section but was unfortunately pretty much always filled with people. Great place to spend the weekend letting the kids immerse themselves. Parking is also pretty reasonable at $1.20 per hour on Sundays.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The Central Public Library in Singapore is a vibrant space blending innovation, design, and functionality. Located at the National Library Building, it offers an extensive collection of books, digital resources, and themed spaces catering to all age groups. Highlights include the eco-friendly My Tree House, the world’s first green library for children, and its lush ambiance. The library’s modern facilities, cozy reading nooks, and seamless technology make it an ideal place for study or leisure. Regular workshops and events add to its community spirit. A hub of knowledge and creativity, it’s a must-visit for book lovers and lifelong learners.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Today, I attended Emma Memma's Performance at Drama Centre. I grew up watching Sesame Street and Hi-5, and of course, The Wiggles. So when I learned that Emma Memma, formerly of The Wiggles, was performing, I was really excited.

What I find especially meaningful is the way Emma Memma and Elvin Melvin connects through sign language. For that, kudos to them.

Some children can only sing and communicate through sign language — yet they’re often overlooked in theatre shows. That’s why Emma Memma’s performances stand out: she reminds us that inclusion matters, and every child deserves to shine. (Catch her in action on youtube.)

Drop by our libraries or check out the NLB online portal and mobile app! You’ll find a huge variety of e-books, e-magazines, audiobooks, and lots of other exciting features. Truly one of the best resources you can enjoy in Singapore 🙂

Have you seen the sky-bridged library, on the upper floors, rising 16 storeys high? Designed with floor-to-ceiling glass panels, it welcomes natural light into every corner, creating a warm, open atmosphere. Look out for the greenery that softens the sleek architecture, blending nature with structure to offer a calming, inviting space.

review_text:
Today, I attended Emma Memma's Performance at Drama Centre. I grew up watching Sesame Street and Hi-5, and of course, The Wiggles. So when I learned that Emma Memma, formerly of The Wiggles, was performing, I was really excited.

What I find especially meaningful is the way Emma Memma and Elvin Melvin connects through sign language. For that, kudos to them.

Some children can only sing and communicate through sign language — yet they’re often overlooked in theatre shows. That’s why Emma Memma’s performances stand out: she reminds us that inclusion matters, and every child deserves to shine. (Catch her in action on youtube.)

Drop by our libraries or check out the NLB online portal and mobile app! You’ll find a huge variety of e-books, e-magazines, audiobooks, and lots of other exciting features. Truly one of the best resources you can enjoy in Singapore 🙂

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries

review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is truly an amazing place and definitely a must-visit attraction in Singapore. The entire area is beautifully designed, extremely clean, and very well maintained, making it a wonderful experience for visitors of all ages.

The Flower Dome and Cloud Forest offer a unique opportunity to see plants and flowers from different parts of the world. During our visit, we also experienced the Tulip Feast, where we saw many beautiful varieties of tulips in full bloom. The colorful display was absolutely stunning and added a vibrant touch to the overall experience. The indoor waterfall inside the Cloud Forest is especially impressive and creates a refreshing atmosphere. The Supertree Grove is another major highlight, and the night light show there feels absolutely magical with the music and colorful lights.

The whole place is spacious and easy to explore, although there is a lot to walk around, so it can feel a little tiring after some time. Still, the beautiful views and attractions make the walking completely worth it.

There are many perfect photo spots throughout the gardens, especially during the evening when the night view looks stunning. Souvenir shops are also available if you would like to buy gifts or memories from your visit.

Overall, it’s a peaceful and beautiful place to relax, enjoy nature, and experience one of Singapore’s most iconic attractions. Highly recommended, especially for first-time visitors.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is truly a highlight of Singapore and absolutely not to be missed. I’d highly recommend getting a combo ticket for the Cloud Forest and Flower Dome—plan for about 2-3 hours to cover both.

The Cloud Forest is spectacular, almost Jurassic Park-like in parts, and enjoyable for both kids and adults. There’s misting at specific times, so it’s worth checking those in advance. A great way to explore is to head to the top level first and then make your way down, stopping at the different exhibits along the way.
The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, showcasing flowers and plants from around the world, with plenty of photo-worthy corners throughout. There’s also a nice gift shop with garden and flower-themed souvenirs.

I also got a separate ticket for the OCBC Skyway and it was 100% worth it. I went up right at 7:45 PM during the light and sound show and got to watch it from the top—an incredible experience. You can stay up for about 15 minutes, and while there’s an elevator, lines to go can get long.

For food, the Jurassic-themed food court nearby is a good option for dinner. Overall, it felt like a well-spent ~$60 to explore all three places. I’d suggest going after 3 PM and spending the entire evening there to fully enjoy the whole area.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Gardens by the Bay is absolutely magical and one of my favorite spots in Singapore. The Supertree Grove is stunning, especially at night when the light and sound show begins—it feels like stepping into a fairy tale. I loved walking through the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest; both are beautifully maintained and full of unique plants and flowers from around the world. The pathways and gardens are perfect for leisurely strolls, and there are so many spots to take photos or just pause and enjoy the scenery. It’s peaceful, inspiring, and feels like a green oasis right in the middle of the city. Whether you’re a nature lover, a photography enthusiast, or just looking for a relaxing escape, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
During my recent visit to Singapore, one of the most memorable places I explored was Gardens by the Bay. This remarkable attraction is a perfect example of how nature, technology, and sustainability can come together to create something truly extraordinary.

From the moment I entered the gardens, I was impressed by the sheer scale and beauty of the place. The landscape is meticulously maintained, featuring a stunning variety of plants, flowers, and themed gardens from around the world. The iconic Supertree Grove is undoubtedly the highlight, with its futuristic structures creating a breathtaking skyline, especially during the evening when they are beautifully illuminated.

I also had the opportunity to visit the conservatories, where the climate-controlled environments showcase unique plant species from different regions. Walking through these attractions felt like traveling across multiple ecosystems in a single day. The attention to detail, cleanliness, and visitor-friendly facilities were outstanding.

What impressed me most was Singapore’s commitment to environmental sustainability, which is reflected throughout the entire garden. Despite being located in the heart of a modern city, the gardens provide a peaceful escape where visitors can relax, take photographs, and appreciate nature.

Whether you are traveling with family, friends, or exploring solo, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit destination. It offers a unique blend of natural beauty, innovation, and world-class design that leaves a lasting impression. My visit was truly unforgettable, and I would highly recommend it to anyone planning a trip to Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is one of Singapore’s most impressive attractions and a place that keeps rewarding repeat visits. What started as a quick visit to the Supertrees has grown into an entire day of exploration, from the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest to the outdoor gardens, lakes, sculptures, and hidden corners scattered throughout the grounds

The Cloud Forest remains the highlight for me. Walking through the cool mist, towering ferns, orchids, and prehistoric-looking plants feels like stepping into another world. The elevated walkways offer fantastic perspectives, and the constantly changing displays make every visit feel fresh. The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, with seasonal floral exhibitions and meticulously maintained landscapes

Beyond the conservatories, there is so much to discover. The Supertrees are iconic, the outdoor gardens are peaceful and surprisingly diverse, and even simple walks around the lakes reveal interesting plants, wildlife, and photo opportunities. I particularly enjoyed wandering through areas that many visitors overlook, including the lily-covered ponds and the themed garden displays

Another pleasant surprise was discovering that the Conservatory Gift Shop leads into a complimentary Jurassic World Experience, which added an unexpected element of fun to the visit. Combined with convenient access via MRT, dining options such as Satay by the Bay, excellent facilities, and world-class landscaping, it is easy to spend an entire day here without getting bored

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Bayfront MRT Station is the quintessential gateway to Singapore’s most iconic modern landmarks, functioning more as a polished underground lobby than a mere transit stop.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Bayfront MRT Station is the quintessential gateway to Singapore’s most iconic modern landmarks, functioning more as a polished underground lobby than a mere transit stop. As a cross-platform interchange between the Circle and Downtown Lines, it offers exceptional connectivity, allowing commuters to switch lines seamlessly without navigating complex tunnels.
​The station’s design prioritizes passenger flow and aesthetics. Its direct underground links to Marina Bay Sands, Gardens by the Bay, and the ArtScience Museum are a masterclass in urban planning, saving visitors from the sweltering tropical heat. The station interior is spacious, well-lit, and spotlessly clean, featuring the charming "When the Ships Come In" mural by Lee Wen, which adds a touch of local artistic heritage to the modern transit space.
​While the station can get incredibly crowded during weekends and peak tourist hours, the wide concourses manage the footfall well. Signage is abundant and clear, making wayfinding easy even for first-time visitors.
​Verdict: Bayfront is efficient, futuristic, and incredibly convenient. It is an essential, high-quality transit hub that perfectly complements the world-class attractions it serves.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
First time, first day in Singapore. Receive assistance from 2 security guards (diections only), friendliness from a stranger, and great service from station personnel. What's not to like. These initial interactions helped my wife and me to fall in love with this city. What a super first impression! Then again I have spent years in NYC and, loved it, until this  Singapore visit. There is no comparison regarding cleanliness, bathroom availability, noise, temperature comfort, orderliness, efficiency,etc, etc.
Andy-in-Ormond, FL, USA

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The MRT station near Gardens by the Bay. You can reach it through the Downtown Line (blue) or the Circle Line (yellow). I love the way they put maps at every intersection inside the station, it’s really helpful for first-timers like us. The signage is clear, and it also lists nearby locations, so you just need to find the name of the place and check which exit to take on the map.

The station area feels really lively, with lots of hobby communities like dance groups, anime fans, and there’s even a piano you can play! The distance to Gardens by the Bay is super close. There’s also a water refill station nearby!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Excellent MRT station, which is part of the very usable,  safe, clean, and easy -to- use MRT system in Singapore.
Added benefit is the wonderful dancing by local girls practicing and perfecting their dance routines  in front of the long mirrors in this MRT  tunnel.
Final take.....enjoy the MRT rides and the entertaining locals buying some great dance moves 👌 👏 .
Recommended.

First time, first day in Singapore. Receive assistance from 2 security guards (diections only), friendliness from a stranger, and great service from station personnel. What's not to like. These initial interactions helped my wife and me to fall in love with this city. What a super first impression! Then again I have spent years in NYC and, loved it, until this  Singapore visit. There is no comparison regarding cleanliness, bathroom availability, noise, temperature comfort, orderliness, efficiency,etc, etc.
Andy-in-Ormond, FL, USA

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The MRT station near Gardens by the Bay. You can reach it through the Downtown Line (blue) or the Circle Line (yellow). I love the way they put maps at every intersection inside the station, it’s really helpful for first-timers like us. The signage is clear, and it also lists nearby locations, so you just need to find the name of the place and check which exit to take on the map.

The station area feels really lively, with lots of hobby communities like dance groups, anime fans, and there’s even a piano you can play! The distance to Gardens by the Bay is super close. There’s also a water refill station nearby!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Excellent MRT station, which is part of the very usable,  safe, clean, and easy -to- use MRT system in Singapore.
Added benefit is the wonderful dancing by local girls practicing and perfecting their dance routines  in front of the long mirrors in this MRT  tunnel.
Final take.....enjoy the MRT rides and the entertaining locals buying some great dance moves 👌 👏 .
Recommended.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Tucked within the vibrant heart of Tekka Food Centre, 蒸好味 Steamed Fish Cuisine is a quiet ode to the beauty of honest, well-executed cooking.

What sets this humble gem apart is its unwavering commitment to freshness — each fish is live-caught for the day, delivering a delicate sweetness that simply cannot be replicated. Gently steamed to perfection, the flesh remains tender and succulent, elevated only by a refined touch of ginger and scallion that enhances rather than overpowers. Every bite speaks of clarity, balance, and respect for the ingredient.

Equally noteworthy is their steamed egg — a silky, comforting masterpiece crafted from real eggs cracked fresh on the spot, not pre-mixed liquid substitutes. The result is a custard-like texture that is impossibly smooth, rich, and deeply satisfying.

In a setting as bustling as Tekka, this stall stands out not through extravagance, but through precision, sincerity, and an unwavering dedication to quality. A true hidden indulgence for those who appreciate the quiet luxury of simplicity done exceptionally well.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The food never disappoints—every bite hits the spot. While the service is decent, there’s still room to elevate the overall experience. If you want to enjoy it at its best, come before lunchtime… unless you don’t mind a long, hungry wait in line.
And once you’re seated, don’t hold back—order the duck webs, pig ears, and duck wings. Paired with an ice-cold beer, it’s the kind of combo that keeps you reaching for more.
One visit is all it takes—you’ll already be planning your next.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The first time I tried the food stalls here was on a Sunday afternoon. It was crowded and I got overwhelmed. I came back on a Wednesday and had tremendous service even though we barely spoke the same language.  Food was delicious. I found a clean spot to sit and eat and soak in the atmosphere.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Actually went there to try again the famous 545 whampoa prawn noodle. Saw this stall (several stall from 545). An old amah alone selling. Surprisingnya the food taste good. And worth the price for a bowl like this.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Did a market walk at Tekka Centre — a true slice of Singapore life! 🇸🇬

First built in 1915 as Kandang Kerbau Market, this Little India landmark has evolved into a vibrant multi-use hub:

Ground/Level 1 – bustling dry/wet market & food centre with fresh produce & multi-ethnic eats - spoilt for choice!

Level 2 – heritage shops with colourful textiles & hidden gems

B1 – convenient parking below

My Favorite stop: Alhajar Latifa (#01-116) — where curry spices are freshly blended just for you. Always greeted by cheerful & pleasant staff.

Will be back to explore more of Level 2 soon!😻

review_text: Restaurant have several hit and miss cuisines but the most recent visit had me trying the fish and fries. It was really good and affordable. The fish was not overly battered and there were thick flakes of fish within that is light and fluffy!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Singapore Swimming Club has a great selection of Indian food and have always enjoyed the food, especially someone who is very discerning. Having said that, off late the quality of paneer dishes seems to be off. The paneer tikka is no longer soft, creamy and flavourful. I think they may have changed their supplier or switched to using frozen paneer. The management should look into it and bring back the original quality paneer.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Been coming here for years since I’ve been a kid. Always love how homely and family-friendly this place is. The eateries are fantastic, especially the cafe and Chinese restaurant. The club sports facilities are excellent, I’ve used the pool, tennis courts, bowling alley, and gym before. With the Katong Park station almost ready nearby, it will become even more accessible.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Went there for the second time as my aunt is their member. The first round tried their lunch buffet. Decent taste in many varieties. This round tried their fried hokkien mee. place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I alighted and later boarded here today (5 March 2026). Earlier, I alighted to visit the nextdoor via Exit A, Sim Lim Square. As I live in the Hillview area, this Downtown Line station is such a convenience to me to go to the Rochor area and Little India - that is, without having to change MRT line. I also noticed that this MRT Station is quite deep underground but getting to the ground level is facilitated by two sets of ascending (or descending) escalators. This MRT Station appears well maintained and clean.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
🟦 Rochor MRT station
11 Rochor Canal Road
● Singapore 188505

🟦 Rochor MRT Station (✅️ DT13)
*************************************
🔵 This is my first visit to Rochor MRT Station. ● This underground Downtown Line station features a spacious concourse and 2 main exits ( ➡🟧  Exit A and Exit B).

🟦 Exit A leads you to Rochor Canal Road, near Sim Lim Square, Burlington Square, Fu Lu Shou Complex, Albert Complex, LASALLE College of the Arts, and nearby shops and hotels. ✔️ It’s the busier primary exit with escalators & llifts.

🟦 Exit B opens toward Sungei Road and nearby residential blocks, Masjid Abdul Gafoor, Rochor Centre, Sim Lim Tower, and the vicinity of Tekka Place/Indian Heritage Centre ( ➡️ 🟡 Bus stop “Bef Rochor Station Exit B”).

🟡 I took Bus 57 from Blk 27 Toa Payoh East and alighted at “Bef Rochor Station Exit B” ----which is a short walk to the station entrance.

🟢 Overall, this is a well-connected and convenient station with good access to shopping, cultural spots and buses.

➡️ 6 Feb 2026 / Friday afternoon

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: It's pretty straightforward, if you're studying or working in the area. If you're looking to shop for electronics, you'd likely need to pass through this MRT station, as it brings you directly to Sim Lim Square. The station is relatively elderly-friendly too, as there's Bencoolen Street nearby.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: This MRT station is within walking distance to many busy areas, situated just beside a few, namely Sim Lim Square to get electronics and devices, Laselle College of the Arts and opposite the Rochor stretch of malls, Tekka Place, Burlington Square Shopping Mall, Indian Heritage Centre. It is also near to Sim Lim Tower, NAFA campuses, OG Albert complex, Bencoolen mall and various hotels. The station’s internal is designed artistically. Overall nice and new appeal.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.
The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Easiest way to take bus to Woodlands checkpoint using Causeway link cross border bus. Only SGD 4.80.

There is also sbs transit and SJE bus operating from this terminal

Accessibility: 400 meter to Bugis MRT (EWL/Green Line/ DWL Blue Line) and also can access to Jalan Besar MRT (DWL/ Blue Line)

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: To get to JB from here is easy. If you want to sit in the bus, line up on the right side but if you don’t kind standing, line up on the left side.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Why is there nowhere to sit?
Now some days later after arriving at Kuala Lumpur’s beautiful bus terminal, the comparison to Singapore’s terminal is extreme. KL’s is like a modern airport terminal, Singapore’s Queen Street is more like a empty boot sale

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Overall, the transportation itself was smooth and we had no issues with the ride. However, our experience at the station was quite disappointing due to the attitude of one of the staff members.
We were traveling with a young child and accidentally walked into the station area because we did not notice the signage, which was quite small and easy to miss. Instead of guiding us in a calm and helpful manner, an elderly staff member raised his voice at us in a way that felt unnecessary and unfriendly.
We completely understand that there are rules to follow, and we had no intention of going the wrong way. A polite explanation would have been much more appropriate, especially considering we were with a child.
We hope the staff can be reminded to communicate more kindly with visitors. A little patience and understanding can make a big difference in creating a welcoming environment for everyone.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The terminal itself is easy to find, but my experience with the security staff was very poor. While crossing a zebra crossing with no buses entering or exiting, a guard revved his engine at me. He then aggressively asked if I could speak English and if I was "blind," pointing to a sign. When I pointed out it was a zebra crossing, he shouted that the rule was only for bus drivers.

According to Singapore's Highway Code, pedestrians have the right of way on pedestrian crossings. The guard's aggressive and incorrect interpretation of the rules makes pedestrians feel unsafe. Management urgently needs to retrain its security team on basic traffic rules and respectful communication.

We hope the staff can be reminded to communicate more kindly with visitors. A little patience and understanding can make a big difference in creating a welcoming environment for everyone.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The terminal itself is easy to find, but my experience with the security staff was very poor. While crossing a zebra crossing with no buses entering or exiting, a guard revved his engine at me. He then aggressively asked if I could speak English and if I was "blind," pointing to a sign. When I pointed out it was a zebra crossing, he shouted that the rule was only for bus drivers.

According to Singapore's Highway Code, pedestrians have the right of way on pedestrian crossings. The guard's aggressive and incorrect interpretation of the rules makes pedestrians feel unsafe. Management urgently needs to retrain its security team on basic traffic rules and respectful communication.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
One of Singapore's most convenient MRT interchange stations. Clean, well-maintained, and easy to navigate with clear signage. The station provides smooth connections between multiple MRT lines, making travel across Singapore fast and convenient.
The facilities are excellent, with lifts, escalators, and barrier-free access for all commuters. Trains are frequent, and the station is generally well organized even during busy hours.
Highly recommended for both daily commuters and visitors exploring Singapore.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
date of visit: 2025-11-21

2 charging points spotted at the purple/ north east line and quite dirty, no cleaning done.

the charging point further from the ticket office does not work, no electricity when plugged in the phone charger.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Outram Park MRT Station is a major transport hub in Singapore, offering seamless connectivity between the East-West (EWL), North-East (NEL), and Thomson-East Coast Lines (TEL). The station is spacious, clean, and well-maintained, reflecting the efficiency of Singapore’s MRT system.

What stands out is the clear signage and accessibility features, including elevators and escalators, which make it easy for everyone to navigate. It’s also conveniently located near key spots like Singapore General Hospital and Chinatown, making it a great starting point for exploring the city.

During peak hours, it can get crowded, but the trains run frequently, so the wait is minimal. Overall, a fantastic and essential MRT station that enhances commuting across Singapore!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Thank you kindly to the many kind and helpful people at Outram mrt today 13/2/2026. I fainted and two bystanders came to assist me, ensuring I was ok and being looked after by staff before they went on their way. place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
One of my favourite AF in town. Split over 2 floors, this is the slightly troublesome part, so bring your keyfob with you at all times. 3rd floor is most of the machines and some half-racks and smiths. 4th floor has 6 full racks, powerlifting platform, dumbbell area with db up to 50kg and 5 benches, and full cable jungle setup. Unique machines like belt squat and combined high row/ chest press combined machine. There is a functional area on 5th floor with another cable stacks.

Overall well maintained and clean. They use a nice essential oil air freshener as well. Showers on the lower floor. I like to drop by for my workout when in town as they have almost everything you need 👍🏻

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Well equipped gym with good crowd. Easy access in good location. Wash rooms x 2 for each gender, one on each level.  One point though, sign is for gym users (your customers) and the language is crude and unsuitable.

Simply a more restorative way - thank you for returning the weights for other users - would be more professional.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Highly recommend! The equipment is top-notch, and the entire three story facility was impeccably clean. The customer service is also fantastic, Ahmad was welcoming and super helpful.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Anytime Fitness Bugis is easily one of the best gyms in the area. The space is clean, well-maintained, and equipped with everything you need — from free weights and functional equipment to a solid range of machines and cardio options. The staff are friendly, helpful, and always make the environment welcoming, even during busy hours.

I especially love how spacious the gym feels despite its central location. It’s easy to find a spot to work out, and the overall vibe is motivating but not overwhelming. The members here are respectful, and the atmosphere feels safe and comfortable for all fitness levels.

The 24/7 access is a huge plus, and the location is super convenient, just a short walk from Bugis MRT. If you’re looking for a clean, accessible, and well-equipped gym in the heart of Bugis, this is definitely the place to go.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
One of the best branches of AF I’ve visited. It’s clean, the equipment are all new and the gym is very spacious, spanning across 2 floors (3 and 4).

I especially appreciate the variety of equipment and number of racks available, compared to other branches.

The only issue is it’s good location, which can cause it to be very crowded most of the time.

Haven’t had any encounters with the staff yet but I assume they are nice as per AF standard.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: The mutation orchid garden is truly stunning and unique, filled with rare and vibrant blooms that capture attention at every corner. Each orchid displays unusual colors, intricate patterns, and elegant shapes, creating a magical atmosphere for visitors. The garden is carefully arranged, allowing guests to walk through and admire the beauty up close. However, the strong sunlight makes the visit slightly tiring, especially during midday. Despite the heat, the breathtaking scenery and peaceful environment make the experience worthwhile. With a bit more shade or a cooler time of day, the visit would be even more enjoyable and relaxing for everyone.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Been here many times.  Still think it is a good place to relax.
The garden is very well planned and full of greenery.
There is also a new indoor garden air-conditioning space where you can enjoy completely different species of orchids.
If it rains, there are many places to hide.  There are also drinks vending machines.
Even if you need to pay in the park, I suggest you come.  It's worth it!👍

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Absolutely spectacular! These garden were Beautiful and very well put together. Clearly a lot of thought has been put into the displays. They also had spots specifically set up for photo opportunities which helps to reduce clogging on the paths. There are bathrooms and places to get refreshments near by. There are also shaded areas throughout the gardens where you can shelter from the rain or sun as needed.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Well manicured and maintained, this place is. The design of this part of Botanic gardens takes you on a journey through meandering pathways and cooling glasshouse. Certainly worth the entrance fee. Orchids are one of the most intriguing flowers. As the old adage goes necessity is the mother of evolution, these orchids are perfectly evolved to benefit from their symbiotic pollinators. Some uses trickery to entice the male species of insects to land on the stigma, some make the landing pad comfortable for a nice sip of delicious nectar. Amazing!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Absolutely stunning the highlight of our trip for me. An incredible number of beautiful orchids in a lovely landscaped and meandering garden. Entry is just 5 dollars making this easily one of the most affordable things to do in Singapore, and absolutely worthwhile. You could easily spend a day wandering the orchid garden and the surrounding botanic gardens

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Everything is amazingly well managed right from the entry to the exit. The environment inside feels like stepping into another world — cool, misty, and beautifully designed. The lush greenery, rare plants, and the stunning indoor waterfall create a magical atmosphere the moment you step in.
I would strongly recommend pre-booking your tickets through Klook to avoid wasting time buying tickets at the entrance. It makes the process much faster and smoother. If you're lucky, you might even get a good discount there, which is definitely a bonus.
The Jurassic Park–themed section was absolutely amazing and added an adventurous vibe to the visit. The dinosaurs looked so realistic, and kids absolutely loved it — they were thrilled throughout.
For me, this was truly a lifetime experience. I lived every bit of it and enjoyed each moment inside. A must-visit attraction in Singapore! 🌿🦖

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
I visited the Cloud Forest at Gardens by the Bay, and it was an amazing experience. The place is incredibly cool—literally and atmospherically—with a refreshing climate that makes it very comfortable to explore.

The variety of plants is impressive, featuring rare and exotic species from tropical highlands around the world. One of the highlights is the massive indoor waterfall, which creates a stunning visual and adds to the calming environment. The entire conservatory is beautifully designed, with elevated walkways that let you explore different levels and enjoy unique views.

It’s a large area, perfect for walking around at your own pace. Despite being a popular attraction, it still feels peaceful and relaxing, making it a great escape from the busy city.

Overall, the Cloud Forest is a must-visit in Singapore—perfect for nature lovers, photography enthusiasts, or anyone looking for a serene and unique experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
We visited the Cloud Forest during the Jurassic World: The Experience, and it made the entire experience even more special and memorable.

From the moment you enter, you’re welcomed by the breathtaking indoor waterfall, which instantly sets the tone. The whole space feels like stepping into a misty rainforest, with cool air, lush greenery, and a peaceful yet dramatic atmosphere. We visited during the misting time, which added an extra layer of magic—the clouds and mist moving through the space made everything feel almost unreal.

The Jurassic World exhibition was very well done and blended naturally into the environment. The dinosaur displays were impressive and fun to discover as you walked through the different levels. It added a sense of adventure, especially as you move along the elevated walkways and explore the dome from different heights.

There was a short wait to go up when we visited, but I can imagine it getting quite busy and the lines becoming longer during peak times. Still, once inside, it’s very easy to take your time and enjoy everything at your own pace.

Everything is beautifully maintained and thoughtfully designed, with plenty of unique photo spots throughout. Between the dramatic waterfall, the misty atmosphere, and the interactive exhibition, it’s a place that feels both relaxing and exciting at the same time.

I would definitely recommend visiting, especially when special exhibitions like Jurassic World are happening—it truly makes the experience even more immersive and unforgettable. 🦖🌿

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: The Cloud Forest dome is more impressive than the Flower Dome. The vertical garden and waterfall are incredibly tall. After exploring the ground floor, you can take the lift up to the very top, then walk your way down via the Cloud Walk. Even though it’s high up, the Cloud Walk doesn’t feel scary at all. Definitely worth a visit.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Even more impressive then I'd thought. This dome inside is awesome inspiring, so vast, with the plants reaching up the the top through the mist. The dome is cool and relaxing atmosphere, plenty to look around and loads of space, so it never feels busy. There are lots of paths, and then a lift up to the top - we were mid morning so no queues, but after we came back down there looked to be a wait. The view down from the top is also very impressive - including out of the dome towards the gardens and 'trees'.
The addition of the Jurassic world dinosaurs added to the whole experience, very well done and strangely fitted on perfectly.
Overall definitely worth a visit.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Chinatown MRT Station (NE4/DT19) is a vibrant and centrally located transit hub in Singapore, serving the North-East Line (NEL) and Downtown Line (DTL). Situated in the heart of Chinatown, it provides easy access to one of Singapore’s most culturally rich and historic neighborhoods.

The station features eye-catching design elements that reflect the heritage of Chinatown, with artwork and motifs inspired by traditional Chinese culture. Its proximity to iconic attractions like the Chinatown Heritage Centre, Buddha Tooth Relic Temple, and Sri Mariamman Temple makes it a popular stop for both tourists and locals.

Chinatown MRT is surrounded by a variety of dining, shopping, and entertainment options, including hawker stalls, souvenir shops, and boutique stores. It’s also well-connected, offering seamless transfers between the NEL and DTL, making it a convenient starting point for exploring other parts of Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.
